hypothesis: Accepting markdown MIME types (text/markdown, text/x-markdown, application/markdown) and passing text/* bodies through verbatim (no tag-stripping) lets enrichment recover sources that a real dogfood run rejected (vercel.com/design.md, 2026-07-09, 'non-text content-type: text/markdown'), without regressing HTML extraction. Predicted: previously-skipped markdown URLs enrich successfully; HTML fetch behavior unchanged.

eval_plan.replay_set: unit fixtures in crates/ovp-enrich/src/web_fetch.rs (markdown + html + binary content types, both feature configs)
eval_plan.live_evidence: .run/p2p3-daily-20260709.log (the rejected vercel.com/design.md skip that motivated this)

rollback: Revert is_texty_content_type/readable_from_body to the strict text/html allowlist; enrichment is idempotent per source and needs-content flags are re-derivable, so no persisted state depends on the wider acceptance.
